text.title: design-tokens-implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-tokens-implementation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Inlining modes inside a single token entry: {"color.bg.primary": { "light": "#fff", "dark": "#111" }}.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inlined modes drift: updating dark without light breaks contrast. No audit trail in git diff. Runtime mode switching requires conditional logic in components.

failure-mode.description: Agents flatten the primitive→semantic→component hierarchy "for simplicity", removing the alias layer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without aliases, mode switching requires editing every component file instead of one semantic token file. The entire point of the pipeline is lost.

failure-mode.description: Agents commit generated CSS into the same repo as sources.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: On the next CI run the generated file matches HEAD and the repo is dirty. The build is non-deterministic. Add dist/ to .gitignore and always commit JSON only.

failure-mode.description: Agent updates dark mode tokens but forgets the light mode pair, breaking contrast ratio.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pair every mode change with an a11y check (axe, jest-dom). WCAG failures on one theme are invisible until tested. Require agents to test both modes.

failure-mode.description: Agents copy Style Dictionary v2 examples (transformGroup, .buildAllPlatforms()) into v3 configs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: v2 and v3 have incompatible APIs. v2 examples fail silently or with cryptic errors. Require agents to read Style Dictionary v3 docs.

failure-mode.description: Agents generate output in both CSS and JavaScript but forget to update the Tailwind config to use the JS output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Duplicated token definitions in Tailwind config and CSS variables cause divergence. Always consume from one output (usually JS for Tailwind, CSS for app HTML).
